search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae atopic dermatitis issue 2 2024 flare history red flags
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic emollient, topical steroid, antimicrobial, dose, duration, referral, or emergency instruction
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Severity requires complete clinician assessment and cannot be inferred from generic selected fields.
No treatment, medicine, dose, referral, or urgency conclusion was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with clinician-established or suspected atopic dermatitis returning with a possible flare; age and pregnancy context remain necessary.
setting_applicability: UAE telehealth, primary, and dermatology follow-up with direct examination when needed.
UAE_applicability: The primary source is UAE DHA guidance; medicine, formulary, facility, infection, emergency, and referral decisions remain clinician- and pathway-specific.
superseded_check: No newer DHA atopic-dermatitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The UAE DHA atopic-dermatitis guideline directly supports documenting itch, dryness, flare course, morphology, distribution, atopy, sleep and quality-of-life impact during an eczema review without inferring current severity or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports recording rapid worsening, pain, fever, lethargy, blistering, infection, mucosal or systemic concern only when assessed, not asserting absence or generating escalation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The management section supports recording a clinician-entered plan, discussion, safety net, and arranged follow-up without generating skin-care, medicine, dose, or referral advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Morphology, distribution, differential, and selected investigation context were reviewed; no diagnosis or test is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact body sites and morphology, trigger and exposure history, treatment and adherence, medicine and dose, response and adverse effects, infection status, diagnosis, severity, investigation, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
